reason: Both answers hit the core points well: moat shifts to workflow depth, data loops, distribution, and switching costs, plus the message that model-quality leads are not durable. They're very close in quality and structure. B edges ahead by including a distinct point (cost/latency/margin engineering via smaller or routed models) that A omits entirely, adding a real dimension of defensibility. B's closing line ("6 months ahead on model capability is worth ~zero... assume it's already gone") is a slightly sharper, more actionable framing of the same idea A expresses more diffusely. A has nice phrasing ("treat models as suppliers, not assets") but its brand/category point overlaps with B's distribution/trust point, so it doesn't add unique coverage. Overall B is marginally more complete and equally concise and correct.
